title: New England Clam Chowder
tagline: Creamy, briny, and loaded with clams and potatoes. The real deal.
description: Classic New England-style chowder with tender clams, potatoes, and bacon in a rich cream broth. Serve in a bread bowl for the full experience.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs Yukon gold potatoes, diced 1/2 inch
2 cans (6.5 oz each) chopped clams, drained (reserve juice)
Salt and white pepper to taste
steps: Cook bacon in a large Dutch oven over medium heat until crispy. Remove to a paper towel, leaving fat in pot.
Add butter to bacon fat. Sauté onion and celery 5 minutes until softened.
Sprinkle flour over vegetables. Stir and cook 2 minutes.
Slowly whisk in clam juice, then milk. Add bay leaves and thyme.
Add potatoes. Bring to a simmer and cook 15-20 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Stir in heavy cream and clams. Heat through 5 minutes—don't boil or clams get rubbery.
Remove bay leaves. Season with salt and white pepper.
Serve topped with reserved bacon and fresh chives.
notes: Clam juice: Use the liquid from the canned clams plus bottled clam juice to reach 2 cups.
Fresh clams: Steam 3 lbs littlenecks. Chop meat, strain and use the cooking liquid.
Potatoes: Yukon Golds hold their shape. Russets make it creamier but can get mushy.
White pepper: Traditional for chowder. Black pepper works but adds visible specks.
Make ahead: Flavors improve overnight. Reheat gently.
